entries.description: Operator set pathAwareCritique: advisory.
Operator answered the UAT/E2E suggested-state prompt: both.
Read the settled verdict, Q8 compat matrix, and Set 087 renderer architecture; routed the required assignment and bounded multi-root assembly decision.
Switched the host snapshot to per-root computeVisibleModules with a pure global merge/payload adapter; added duplicate-name winner payload and one notification per fresh cache cycle.

Deleted the implicit-only flat branch; every repo state now renders module -> bucket -> row with stable testids, muted auto-expanded Default, fallback warnings, and duplicate-name badge/tooltip. Renamed the contributed label to Work Explorer with the view ID unchanged.
Updated Layer 2 source pins, Playwright fixtures, live README/onboarding names, and regenerated the hero screenshot from the Work Explorer mockup.

Focused validation passed: 61 Layer 2 tests, extension compile/typecheck, six initial Layer 3 passes plus repaired stale bucket assertion, manifest-less rerun green, and duplicate-name multi-root Playwright green.

Canonical full pass: Python 2,922 passed / 6 skipped; guidance ceilings OK; TypeScript typecheck + Layer 2 1,413 passed; Layer 3 21 passed after updating stale flat-tree selectors; local 0.41.0 VSIX packaged for armed UAT.
Operator completed and saved the ad-hoc checklist, then explicitly confirmed acceptance: all three substantive Work Explorer UAT walks passed; blank Other rows are optional feedback slots.

After two Anthropic HTTP 400 attempts, the sanctioned remaining-provider fallback ran through the canonical stamped verification pipeline on Google gemini-3-1-pro: VERIFIED, zero findings, two non-blocking nits. This was round 1, before the final ai-assignment actuals write.

Anthropic Opus round 2 restored freshness against the final work state and returned ISSUES_FOUND: one Major inferred that a retained negative Layer 2 client guard contradicted the single-dialect renderer and likely made the suite red.

Inspected the full guard body: the predicted red test was disproven, but its title and negative intent were stale. Replaced it with a positive semantic-input/no-recomputation contract, corrected adjacent legacy-helper wording, and passed the focused 5-test renderer suite plus all 1,413 Layer 2 tests.
Final permitted Anthropic Opus confirmation reviewed the corrected work state: VERIFIED, zero findings. Session 1 verification is terminal and non-blocking.
Registered Session 2 as GitHub Copilot/OpenAI gpt-5.4 high; routed and saved the required bounded assignment/architecture analysis.

Implemented the persistent fault-only System Status strip, relocated environment/workspace diagnostics from the form, and added per-root last-known-good rendering for invalid docs/modules.yaml without file writes.
Validation checkpoint passed: extension compile and TypeScript noEmit; 1,405 Layer 2 tests; all 22 Playwright Electron tests including automatic valid-to-invalid-to-repaired manifest recovery.

Mid-session orchestrator transition per the routed recommendation: resumed as claude-code/anthropic claude-fable-5 medium via idempotent start_session re-attach; preserved all uncommitted implementation work.

Removed the dead form-warning helpers (envWarningHtml/pythonWarningHtml/copilotWarningHtml, their TEXT constants, gs-warning CSS, and the unused transportProfileBlockHtml parameter) and corrected every stale comment echo; routed the required Set 093 orchestrator recommendation (gemini-pro analysis, saved raw in s2-next-set-analysis.json).

Canonical full pass green after cleanup: Python 2,922 passed / 6 skipped; guidance ceilings OK; tsc --noEmit clean; 1,405 Layer 2 tests passed; all 22 Playwright Electron tests passed including the manifest edit -> invalidate -> repair recovery; 0.41.0 local VSIX repackaged with the Session 2 code and systemStatusHtml.js confirmed inside.

Authored the Session 2 walks (4: healthy no-strip; 5: modules.yaml break-and-repair with last-known-good retention and file-untouched check; 6: cold-start workspace-init fault in the strip over a warning-free form) into the set checklist, preserving the operator-saved Session 1 results. Every non-judgment item carries ProgrammaticVerification; the rebuilt 0.41.0 local VSIX is the walk target. The Session 2 human walk is pending the operator, who is not live in this autonomous continuation.

Round 1 (gpt-5-6, anthropic excluded) returned one Major: the System Status strip rendered from pre-seed gsState on the first getting-started paint, so a durable Lightweight seed could show a false provider-key fault (or hide a real Copilot one). Fixed by computing the strip after the durable seed application and giving list mode its own host-field render; added a source-ordering pin test. Layer 2 green at 1,406.

Round 2 (gpt-5-6, anthropic excluded, complexity 85) reviewed the remediated working tree with the R1 fix, the source-ordering pin, and the literal fault-copy pins: VERIFIED, zero findings. Session 2 verification is terminal and non-blocking; disposition verdict patched to VERIFIED. Playwright re-ran 22/22 after the fix and the 0.41.0 local VSIX was repackaged from the fixed tree.

Automated pull_critique ran the advisory multi-provider path-aware critique (openai gpt-5.4 + google gemini-2.5-pro): gemini VERIFIED; gpt-5.4 ISSUES_FOUND with two findings, both fixed before close - the pending change-log.md was authored and the stale README inline-warning claims were rewritten to describe the System Status strip, with every comment echo in the protocol and client sources swept in the same pass. Artifact saved raw as path-aware-critique.json; per-finding adjudication recorded in disposition.json.

Authored the end-of-set change-log.md, rewrote disposition.json for the final session (verdict VERIFIED preserved from the verify_session patch; next_orchestrator null; lessons L-064-9, L-064-12, L-065-1 cited), and filled the Session 2 actuals plus the routed Set 093 recommendation in ai-assignment.md.
